figures.caption: Figure 1: A t-SNE visualization of harmful comment embeddings generated by our framework, showing detection results from four detection models. Each point represents a generated comment, colored blue if detected as harmful by a given system and gray if missed. The plots visualize the detection patterns and blind spots of each classifier on our generated content.
Table 11: Prompt used by the harmful agent for the w/ persona condition.
Table 13: Prompt for evaluator models.
Table 10: Prompt used by the harmful agent for the extrinsic only condition.

Figure 2: A t-SNE visualization comparing the embedding space of our synthetic scenarios (Ours, bottomright) with eight static benchmarks. The wide distribution of our data visually corroborates its high diversity.

Figure 3: An aspect-wise analysis visualizing generated harmful comment embeddings. The plots compare a model w/o persona (top-left) against configurations utilizing intrinsic aspects only (top-right), extrinsic aspects only (bottom-left), and both aspects combined (bottomright).
Table 7: Prompt used for the profile generation model.
Table 8: Prompt used by the harmful agent for the w/o persona condition.
Table 9: Prompt used by the harmful agent for the intrinsic only condition.

Table 18: Harmful content detection performance (accuracy, %) of detection models on trolling-oriented generations. “Ours” reports generations produced by GPT-4o, Llama-3.1 70B, and DeepSeek-Llama 70B. A lower score indicates a more challenging evaluation set. Scores for our scenarios are underlined if they are lower than all static benchmark scores for that model. Values in parentheses denote standard deviations over five runs.
Table 20: Analysis of linguistic and categorical diversity for non-English harmful scenarios.

Table 4: Comparison of linguistic and categorical diversity for harmful comments generated with and without personas under the trolling-oriented setting. Bold values indicate the better value within each model according to the metric direction.
Table 5: Comparison of linguistic and categorical diversity for harmful comments generated with and without personas under the CADD-based setting.
Figure 4: Distribution of top-visited subreddit categories across user types.
Table 17: Prompt used for harmful content type classification.
Table 6: Case study on persona-based harmful comments. Underlined text indicates the link between specific details in the persona’s intrinsic aspect and the resulting comment’s content.
Table 21: Examples of synthesized intrinsic profiles across different user types.
Table 16: Descriptions of harmful content categories used for evaluator-based classification, following Markov et al. (2023).
Table 15: Prompt used for trolling type classification.
Table 14: Descriptions of trolling types used for classification by the evaluator agent.
